title: PubSub Audit Chain Broadcast and Agent STH Cache

story.as_a: agent participating in a tenant's chain of custody
story.i_want_to: subscribe to real-time audit chain events for my tenant and maintain my own local cache of the latest STH, so I can detect if the server-reported chain diverges from what I saw
story.so_that: I provide independent tamper evidence — a single operator trying to rewrite the chain would have to simultaneously rewrite every connected agent's cached view

rationale: The witness protocol (section 4.4) makes audit tampering detectable by cross-referencing every agent's local cached STH. This story ships the broadcast mechanism and the client-side cache. US-26.5.2 adds the header validation that ties the cache into request handling.

acceptance_criteria.description: `Loopctl.AuditChain.append/2` (from US-26.1.1) publishes every new entry to Phoenix.PubSub topic `audit_chain:<tenant_id>` after the successful database commit. Publishing happens outside the transaction to avoid blocking the append on subscriber fanout.

The published message includes: chain_position, entry_hash, action, entity_type, entity_id, actor_lineage (for consumers to correlate with their own operations). Payload bytes are not included — clients can fetch them if needed.
New Oban worker `Loopctl.Workers.BroadcastSthWorker` runs when a new STH is computed (US-26.1.2) and publishes the STH to the same PubSub topic as a separate event type `{type: :sth_updated, ...}`.
mcp-server (loopctl-mcp-server package) gains a WebSocket or HTTP long-poll subscriber for the audit_chain topic. Subscriber connects on MCP session start using the authenticated api_key.
mcp-server maintains an in-memory cache per session: `{latest_sth, recent_entry_hashes (last 1000)}`. Cache is updated as events arrive.

On reconnect after a disconnect, mcp-server requests a reconciliation batch: `GET /api/v1/audit/chain/since/:last_position?tenant_id=...` returns entries since the last known position. Cache is updated.
A helper function in mcp-server exposes the current cached STH so other tools can read it and attach it to outbound requests (for US-26.5.2).

Fanout scales to at least 50 concurrent agents per tenant without significant latency. Load-test documents the ceiling and the failure mode (slow subscribers get a circuit breaker that drops their subscription — they reconcile on reconnect).
The topic is per-tenant, so cross-tenant subscribers are impossible. Enforced by Phoenix.PubSub topic scoping + the subscribing channel's authentication check.
System article stub `witness-protocol` updated (full content in US-26.4.5) to reference this mechanism. CHANGELOG entry.

test_cases.name: Append triggers PubSub broadcast
expected_results: Subscriber receives a message with the new chain_position and entry_hash

test_cases.name: mcp-server cache updates on event receipt
steps: Inject a fake audit event into the cache's event loop

test_cases.name: Reconnection fetches missed entries
preconditions: mcp-server session disconnects at position 5, chain now at position 10

technical_notes: Phoenix.PubSub: use the existing Phoenix.PubSub setup from Epic 17 (orchestrator observability).
mcp-server cache: simple JavaScript object with TTL on entries. No external dep needed.
Subscription protocol: loopctl's existing MCP channel or a new WebSocket endpoint. Prefer extending existing channel.
Reconciliation endpoint: new `GET /audit/chain/since/:position` in the audit controller.
